summary: Markets closed out Friday with fresh all-time highs as the S&P 500 rose 0.29% to 7,230 and the Nasdaq surged 0.89% to 25,114, capping their best month since 2020. The Fed held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29 amid a dramatic 8-4 split vote, with four dissents reflecting deep internal conflict over inflation risks and easing bias. Powell announced he will stay on as a board governor after his chair term ends May 15, while Kevin Warsh's nomination cleared committee. Energy remains the dominant macro risk as Brent crude hit $111 and WTI reached $105, with oil up 12% for the week on Trump's vow to maintain the Iran blockade. On the crypto front, Bitcoin traded near $77,000 with April spot ETF inflows of $2.44 billion marking the strongest month since October 2025. Whale wallets accumulated even as the Fear & Greed Index sat at 26, while negative funding rates suggest institutional hedging rather than bearish sentiment. Magnificent Seven earnings drove tech leadership, with Apple jumping 3% on strong iPhone sales and upbeat guidance, while Q1 S&P 500 earnings growth surged 31% year-over-year, beating estimates by 23%. The combination of resilient earnings, record AI capex, and hopes for Iran ceasefire progress has propelled equities despite elevated oil and Fed hawkishness. Rate cuts remain off the table until 2027, yet markets are pricing in a soft landing with equity allocations trumping macro caution.

signals.title: Fed holds rates with unprecedented 4-dissent vote, signals no cuts until 2027
signals.desc: The FOMC kept rates at 3.5%-3.75% in an 8-4 decision on April 29, the most dissents since 1992, as three members opposed easing bias language and inflation remains elevated from Middle East energy shocks—markets now price no cuts until late 2027.

signals.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record highs on strong Q1 earnings surge
signals.desc: The S&P 500 rose to 7,230 and Nasdaq to 25,114 on May 1 as 80% of reporting companies beat EPS estimates with 31% year-over-year growth, led by tech, financials, and communication services—fueling optimism despite valuation concerns.

signals.title: Oil surges 12% for week as Trump vows to maintain Iran blockade
signals.desc: Brent crude hit $111 and WTI reached $105 on May 1 as President Trump confirmed the U.S. naval blockade on Iranian ports will continue, keeping the Strait of Hormuz largely closed and threatening to push inflation higher if the standoff persists.

signals.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit $2.44B in April, strongest since October 2025
signals.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $2.44 billion in net inflows during April with BlackRock's IBIT capturing 70%, while BTC traded near $77,000 and whale wallets holding 1,000+ BTC increased by 142 addresses over six months despite Fear & Greed at 26.

signals.title: Apple beats estimates, jumps 3% on strong iPhone demand and China momentum
signals.desc: Apple posted fiscal Q2 EPS of $2.01 vs. $1.95 estimate and revenue beat on May 1, with robust iPhone sales and upbeat current-quarter guidance overshadowing a revenue miss—shares rallied over 3% and powered the Nasdaq to a fresh record.

verdict: Equities are pricing in a soft landing with record earnings and AI spending offsetting Fed hawkishness and oil shock risks, but stretched valuations and geopolitical uncertainty leave little margin for error.
